skill.name: biorxiv-database
skill.description: Search, retrieve metadata, and download PDFs for bioRxiv preprints by keywords, authors, or date ranges.
skill.summary: Discover biology preprints from bioRxiv with automated metadata retrieval and PDF download.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 48 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference license identifiers and markdown section text, not actual cryptographic code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are bash command examples in markdown code blocks. The 'hardcoded URL' and 'hardcoded IP' findings point to legitimate bioRxiv API endpoints and a DOI example string. The 'system reconnaissance' findings are in the audit results JSON metadata file, not in skill code. The skill is a legitimate bioRxiv preprint search and PDF download tool with no evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Researchers spend significant time manually searching for recent biology preprints across multiple sources. This skill automates bioRxiv discovery by searching keywords, returning structured metadata, and downloading full-text PDFs in one workflow.
actual_capabilities: Search bioRxiv preprints by keyword with configurable date range lookback
Retrieve structured metadata including title, abstract, DOI, and authors
Download full-text PDFs for preprints identified by their DOI
Filter search results client-side for precise keyword-to-content matching
Operate as a reusable Python API or as a command-line tool
limitations: Keyword filtering is performed client-side after API response, not by the server
PDF download assumes version 1 of the preprint DOI by default
Does not support full-text search of preprint body content
Limited to bioRxiv only (not medRxiv, arXiv, or other preprint servers)

use_cases.title: Weekly Literature Monitoring
use_cases.description: Set up recurring searches for new preprints on a specific research topic and collect metadata for review.
use_cases.target_user: Research scientist tracking new publications in their field

use_cases.title: Systematic Evidence Collection
use_cases.description: Search bioRxiv for papers matching specific keywords within a defined date range and download relevant PDFs.
use_cases.target_user: Systematic review team conducting evidence synthesis

use_cases.title: Rapid Topic Exploration
use_cases.description: Quickly discover what has been published on bioRxiv about a new research topic without manual browsing.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate student exploring a new research area

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for recent preprints about [topic] from the last [N] days. Show me the titles and DOIs of the results.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Keyword Search with Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Find papers about [topic1] and [topic2] on bioRxiv from the past [N] days. List each result with title, DOI, authors, and a short excerpt from the abstract.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher comparing topics across recent literature

prompt_templates.title: Search and Download PDFs
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for [topic] published in the last [N] days. Download the top 3 most relevant PDFs and save them with filenames based on their titles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher building a local paper library

prompt_templates.title: Automated Literature Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a script that searches bioRxiv weekly for [topic], downloads any new PDFs not already collected, and maintains a metadata index file with title, DOI, and download date for each paper.
prompt_templates.scenario: Lab team automating their literature monitoring workflow

output_examples.input: Search bioRxiv for CRISPR papers from the last 7 days.
output_examples.output: Found 12 papers matching your query. Top result: 'CRISPR-based gene editing in human cells' by Smith et al. DOI: 10.1101/2024.01.15.575901. Would you like me to download the full PDF?

output_examples.input: Find recent papers about immunotherapy from the last 30 days and download the PDFs.
output_examples.output: Found 8 papers matching 'immunotherapy'. Downloaded PDF for: 'Advances in CAR-T cell therapy for solid tumors' by Chen et al. File saved to immunotherapy_car_t_2024.pdf. A full metadata summary has been compiled.

best_practices: Verify DOIs before downloading PDFs to avoid unnecessary network requests for invalid identifiers
Use specific, narrow keywords to reduce client-side filtering time on large result sets
Set a reasonable days_back value based on the expected publication frequency for your topic
anti_patterns: Searching with very broad keywords that return hundreds of results, causing slow client-side filtering
Using this skill for medRxiv or other non-bioRxiv preprint databases (it only supports bioRxiv)
Downloading PDFs without first reviewing metadata to confirm relevance to your research question

faq.question: What is bioRxiv?
faq.answer: bioRxiv is a free online archive and distribution service for preprints in the life sciences, operated by Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory.

faq.question: What data can this skill retrieve?
faq.answer: It retrieves preprint metadata including title, abstract, DOI, authors, and publication date from the bioRxiv API.

faq.question: Does this skill download full PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, it can download full-text PDFs for preprints using the preprint DOI. PDFs are saved to a local file path you specify.

faq.question: How does the keyword search work?
faq.answer: The skill fetches recent preprint metadata from the bioRxiv API and filters results on your machine by matching keywords against titles and abstracts.

faq.question: What are the system requirements?
faq.answer: Python 3.8 or later with the requests library installed. Install with: pip install requests.

faq.question: How far back can I search?
faq.answer: The search window is configurable using the days_back parameter. Larger date ranges return more results and may take longer to filter.
